histogram_plot.caption: Histogram of day_since_last_order across 5,630 customers. Orange = mean, purple = median. The gap between them confirms skew direction: mean > median ⇒ right-skewed, mean < median ⇒ left-skewed.

boxplot.caption: Box plot reads as: box = 25th–75th percentile (the middle 50%), line inside box = median, whiskers extend to 1.5 × IQR, dots beyond = outliers. Large vertical separation between the two medians = strong churn predictor. Box overlap that is still high means the feature alone does NOT distinguish the two groups.

mann_whitney_u.narration: Mann-Whitney U = 2,831,473. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature.

cohens_d.narration: Cohen's d = -0.42 — small effect size. Values are higher in retained customers (negative shift).

outliers.narration: 62 outliers by the IQR rule (1.1% of rows). p99 = 15.00, max = 46.00. Severity: moderate. Tree-based models (XGBoost, LightGBM) handle this natively — no action needed. For scale-sensitive models (MLP, Logistic Regression), consider log1p transform at the Stage 3.2 feature-engineering step.

narrative.distribution: Distribution is strongly right-skewed — most values clustered on the low end with a long right-side tail (skewness = +1.27). Mean (4.46) > median (3.00) — confirms the right skew.
narrative.boxplot: Churned median (2.50) is 0.50 lower than retained median (3.00). A weak separator — some shift but substantial overlap between groups.
narrative.separation: Mann-Whitney U = 2,831,473. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature. Cohen's d = -0.42 — small effect size. Values are higher in retained customers (negative shift).
narrative.outliers: 62 outliers by the IQR rule (1.1% of rows). p99 = 15.00, max = 46.00. Severity: moderate. Tree-based models (XGBoost, LightGBM) handle this natively — no action needed. For scale-sensitive models (MLP, Logistic Regression), consider log1p transform at the Stage 3.2 feature-engineering step.

recommendation.transform_reason: distribution close to symmetric OR outlier mass minimal
recommendation.outlier_action: Stage 1 clip is sufficient; trees handle raw values fine
recommendation.rationale: Signal: weak (|Cohen's d| = 0.42, p = 1.071e-41). Transform: none — distribution close to symmetric OR outlier mass minimal. Outlier handling: Stage 1 clip is sufficient; trees handle raw values fine.
